candidates.title: An Algorithmic Proof of the Lovasz Local Lemma via Resampling Oracles
candidates.summary: The Lovasz Local Lemma is a seminal result in probabilistic combinatorics. manual user-curated reading list

candidates.title: Code as Agent Harness
candidates.authors: Xuying Ning, Katherine Tieu, Dongqi Fu, Tianxin Wei, Zihao Li, et al.
candidates.summary: Survey of code as infrastructure for AI agents: harness interfaces, mechanisms, scaling, applications in coding/GUI/embodied/scientific discovery.

candidates.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Rethinking Retrieval for Agentic Search via Direct Corpus Interaction
candidates.authors: Zhuofeng Li, Haoxiang Zhang, et al.
candidates.summary: Direct Corpus Interaction: agents use grep/file-reads on raw corpora, outperforming embedding-based retrieval. Resolution-of-interface argument.

candidates.title: Harnessing Agentic Evolution
candidates.authors: Jiayi Zhang, Yongfeng Gu, et al.
candidates.summary: AEvo: meta-editing framework where a meta-agent edits procedure/context to steer agentic evolution. 26% gain over strongest baseline.

candidates.title: Frontier Coding Agents Implement an AlphaZero Self-Play Pipeline for Connect Four
candidates.authors: Joshua Sherwood, Ben Aybar, Benjamin Kaplan
candidates.summary: Benchmark for agents implementing full ML pipelines autonomously. Claude Opus 4.7 beat Pascal Pons solver 7/8 trials.

candidates.title: Agentic Harness Engineering: Observability-Driven Automatic Evolution of Coding-Agent Harnesses
candidates.authors: Jiahang Lin, Shichun Liu, Chengjun Pan, et al.
candidates.summary: AHE: closed-loop harness evolution via component/experience/decision observability. 77.0 pass@1 Terminal-Bench 2. Branch-named analog.

candidates.title: ASI-Evolve: AI Accelerates AI
candidates.authors: Weixian Xu, Tiantian Mi, et al.
candidates.summary: Agentic framework with learn-design-experiment-analyze cycle autonomously driving AI research in architectures + RL algorithms.

candidates.title: OpenAI gpt-5 disproves a discrete-geometry conjecture (unit-distance bound)
candidates.summary: OpenAI announces gpt-5 disproved a unit-distance / discrete-geometry conjecture. Directly relevant to math-by-AI agent class.

candidates.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems (RecursiveMAS)
candidates.summary: Scaling agent collaboration through latent-space recursion. Official paper impl; HF 1st paper of the week.
